meanings.meaning: I cleanse my mind with the dust of my Guru's lotus feet and describe the pure glory of Lord Rama, which bestows the four fruits of life.
Knowing myself to be ignorant, I meditate on Hanuman and ask for strength, wisdom, and knowledge while removing all suffering.
Victory to Hanuman, the ocean of wisdom and virtue, who illuminates all three worlds.
Messenger of Rama, possessing immense strength, and known as the son of Anjani and the Wind God.
A great hero with immense power, remover of evil thoughts and companion of the wise.
Golden in appearance, beautifully adorned, with earrings and curly hair.
Holding a thunderbolt and a flag, with the sacred thread on your shoulder.
Son of Shiva and Kesari, whose glory is worshipped across the universe.
Wise, virtuous, and intelligent, always eager to serve Lord Rama.
Delighting in hearing Rama’s deeds, with Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita residing in your heart.
You took a subtle form to appear before Sita and a fierce form to burn Lanka.
You assumed a powerful form to destroy demons and complete Rama’s tasks.
You brought the life-saving herb and revived Lakshmana; Rama embraced you with joy.
Rama praised you highly and said you are as dear as his brother Bharata.
Thousands sing your glory; Lord Vishnu embraced you in praise.
Great sages and divine beings like Narada and Saraswati praise you.
Even gods and learned people cannot fully describe your greatness.
You helped Sugriva regain his kingdom by uniting him with Rama.
Vibhishana followed your advice and became king of Lanka.
You leaped towards the sun, thinking it to be a sweet fruit.
Holding Rama’s ring, you crossed the ocean effortlessly.
All difficult tasks become easy with your grace.
You guard Rama’s door; none can enter without your permission.
Those who take refuge in you find happiness and fear nothing.
You control your power; your roar shakes all three worlds.
Evil spirits stay away when your name is recited.
Diseases and suffering are removed by chanting your name.
Hanuman rescues those who remember him with devotion.
Rama is supreme, and you accomplish all his tasks.
Those who pray to you achieve their desires and life’s rewards.
Your glory shines across all ages and illuminates the world.
Protector of saints and destroyer of demons, beloved of Rama.
You grant spiritual powers and treasures, as blessed by Sita.
You possess devotion to Rama and remain his eternal servant.
Through devotion to you, one attains Rama and forgets all sorrows.
At life’s end, one reaches Rama’s abode and is reborn as his devotee.
No need to worship others; serving Hanuman gives all happiness.
All troubles and pain are removed by remembering Hanuman.
Victory to Hanuman; bless me like a compassionate Guru.
Reciting this 100 times frees one from bondage and grants joy.
Reading Hanuman Chalisa brings success, with Lord Shiva as witness.
Tulsidas remains the servant of God; may the Lord reside in my heart.
O son of the Wind, remover of suffering, reside in my heart with Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita.
